baseUrl in tsconfig is relative to tsconfig location not project root
body:
`baseUrl` in tsconfig.json is resolved relative to the tsconfig.json file itself, not to where you run `tsc`. In monorepos with nested tsconfigs, this is a common source of confusion.

```
monorepo/
  packages/
    app/
      tsconfig.json   ← baseUrl: "." means packages/app/
      src/
        index.ts
  tsconfig.base.json  ← baseUrl: "." means monorepo/
```

```json
// packages/app/tsconfig.json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "baseUrl": "."  // Relative to THIS file = packages/app/
  }
}
```

```typescript
// packages/app/src/index.ts
import { foo } from 'src/utils'; // Resolves to packages/app/src/utils
// NOT monorepo/src/utils
```

When extending a base tsconfig, `baseUrl` from the base is relative to the base file. If the child overrides `baseUrl`, it's relative to the child. This is also why `paths` may break when extending configs — paths are resolved relative to `baseUrl`.
